decisions: CMS content stored in structured collections.
Media assets served via CDN.
Public assets should be delivered through a CDN.
Use SSR or ISR for SEO-sensitive public pages when applicable.
Content models must support locale-aware fields and fallback rules.
Scheduled publishing requires a background worker.
[cache-invalidation] On-demand ISR revalidation: Trigger Next.js revalidation via webhook after each publish event. Pages update within seconds. Requires a revalidation API route.

[preview-vs-production] Payload draft mode + Next.js preview: Use Payload's built-in draft mode with Next.js preview cookies. Editors see drafts in-context; public visitors see only published content.
[seo-vs-auth-routes] Route groups with middleware: Use Next.js route groups: (public) for SEO pages, (app) for admin, (payload) for CMS. Middleware enforces auth only on non-public groups.
[media-storage-strategy] local filesystem
[content-versioning] No versioning for MVP: Skip versioning initially. Editors edit content directly. Simpler but no rollback capability.
[i18n-strategy] Full i18n (content + UI + routing): Content, UI labels, and URL structure are all locale-aware. Complete solution but more work upfront.
[job-infrastructure] Separate worker process: Dedicated worker process with its own lifecycle. More reliable. Can scale independently. Needs docker-compose entry.
[auth-strategy] Email + password (built-in): Classic email/password auth. Use the framework's built-in auth if available (e.g., Payload auth). Simple, no external dependencies.
Use SSR or ISR for SEO-sensitive pages.
Serve static assets through CDN.
Implement structured logging.
Add health check endpoints.
Use connection pooling.
Add automated database backups.
Introduce background worker for scheduled tasks.
Store media in S3-compatible object storage.
Media assets stored in object storage.
Background worker processes scheduled jobs.
Authentication handled via secure session or JWT.
Authorization must enforce role and permission boundaries.
Public-facing pages should use caching and delivery strategies appropriate for anonymous traffic.
SEO-sensitive public pages should use rendering strategies such as SSR or ISR when beneficial.
Automation workflows require a background worker and durable job execution strategy.
Introduce caching for frequently accessed public content.
CDN recommended for public assets.
Add monitoring and logging stack.

communication.pattern: REST via Payload auto-generated API + custom endpoints
communication.auth: JWT bearer token or session cookie (Payload built-in)

communication.pattern: Drizzle ORM (Payload built-in) over postgres connection pool
S3-compatible API for media upload and retrieval
Direct postgres connection for job execution
Edge cache proxying static assets and SSR/ISR responses

backend: Content model definition via Payload collections
Media asset management and storage orchestration
Locale-aware data storage and API responses

stories.title: Define CMS content model
stories.description: Define CMS collections, media handling, and editorial workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Content collections are defined with required fields and validation
Content items can be created, read, updated, and deleted via the admin
Media handling is configured for image and file uploads
Payload collections are registered in payload.config.ts
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement draft/publish workflow — that is a separate story
Do NOT implement media library UI — focus on the content model and API
Do NOT implement public-facing content delivery

validation.manual_check: Can create and retrieve a content item via admin or API

stories.title: Configure CDN
stories.description: Configure CDN for public site asset delivery.
acceptance_criteria: Public assets are served through a CDN or edge cache
Cache headers are configured for static assets
Public routes are accessible without authentication
CDN configuration does not break authenticated admin routes
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement the full public site layout — only the delivery infrastructure
Do NOT implement SEO optimizations in this story
expected_files: next.config.ts (updated with caching/CDN headers if applicable)

validation.manual_check: Public route loads and static assets have appropriate cache headers

stories.title: Add locale support
stories.description: Add locale fields and fallback rules to content models.
acceptance_criteria: Content collections support locale-specific fields
Fallback rules are defined for missing translations
Admin interface allows editing content per locale
API responses include locale metadata
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement locale routing in this story — that is separate
Do NOT implement machine translation

validation.manual_check: Can create content in multiple locales via admin

stories.title: Implement locale routing
stories.description: Add locale-aware routing in the frontend experience.
acceptance_criteria: URLs include locale prefix (e.g., /en/..., /pt/...)
Correct locale content is rendered per route
Default locale is used when no prefix is provided
Locale can be switched without losing page context
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement locale detection from browser headers
Do NOT implement per-user locale preferences

validation.manual_check: Can navigate /en/ and /pt/ and see different content

stories.title: Setup job worker
stories.description: Create worker process responsible for scheduled publishing.
acceptance_criteria: Worker process starts and connects to the database
Worker runs on a configurable interval
Worker logs execution start and completion
Worker does not block the main application server
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement specific job logic — only the worker infrastructure
Do NOT implement job management UI

validation.manual_check: Worker process starts and logs heartbeat

stories.title: Implement publishing scheduler
acceptance_criteria: Content with a scheduled publish date is published automatically
Scheduler checks for due content on each run
Published content is visible on the appropriate surface
Failed publish attempts are logged
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement scheduled unpublishing
Do NOT implement complex retry logic — simple retry on next run is sufficient

validation.manual_check: Set publishAt to near future, verify content publishes automatically

stories.title: Initialize project repository
stories.description: Create project structure using nextjs + payload + postgres.
acceptance_criteria: package.json exists with scripts: dev, build, lint, test
.env.example exists with all required environment variables
TypeScript is configured with tsconfig.json
ESLint and Prettier are configured
npm install completes without errors
npm run build passes
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement any features yet — this is project scaffolding only
Do NOT add authentication, roles, or any business logic
Do NOT create database tables — that is a separate story

validation.manual_check: Project directory exists with expected structure

stories.title: Setup database
stories.description: Configure postgres database with connection pool and migrations.
acceptance_criteria: postgres connection is configured via environment variable
Database client or ORM is installed and configured
Initial migration or schema setup runs without errors
Connection can be verified programmatically
docker-compose.yml includes database service
scope_boundaries: Do NOT create application tables yet — only the connection layer
Do NOT add seed data
Do NOT implement any API endpoints

validation.manual_check: Database container starts and connection succeeds

stories.title: Setup backend service
stories.description: Initialize backend using payload with database connection.
acceptance_criteria: Backend is initialized using payload
Server starts and responds to health check
API is accessible at the configured port
Payload config file exists with database adapter configured
Payload admin panel is accessible at /admin
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement authentication yet
Do NOT create domain collections or models yet
Do NOT add business logic — only the backend skeleton

stories.title: Create frontend application
stories.description: Implement frontend application shell using nextjs.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend application renders using nextjs
Root page loads without errors
Basic layout component exists (shell, navigation placeholder)
Next.js app router is configured with route groups
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement pages beyond the root layout and a placeholder home page
Do NOT add authentication UI — that is a separate story
Do NOT style extensively — basic layout only

validation.manual_check: Frontend loads in browser without errors

stories.title: Implement authentication
stories.description: Add registration, login, logout and session management.
acceptance_criteria: Users can register with email and password
Users can log in and receive a valid session
Users can log out and have their session invalidated
Protected routes return 401 for unauthenticated requests
Passwords are hashed before storage
Payload Users collection has auth enabled
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement OAuth or social login in this story
Do NOT implement password reset or email verification
Do NOT implement MFA

validation.manual_check: Can register, log in, and access protected route

stories.title: Implement role-based access control
stories.description: Define roles, permissions, and enforce authorization boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Role model is defined with at least admin and default user roles
Permissions are enforced on protected endpoints
Admin users can manage other users' roles
Unauthorized role access returns 403
Payload access control functions use role checks
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement granular per-field permissions unless required by spec
Do NOT implement a permissions admin UI — enforce in backend only

validation.manual_check: Admin can access admin routes; regular user gets 403

stories.title: Implement media library
stories.description: Allow uploading, listing, and managing media assets.
acceptance_criteria: Users can upload images and files
Uploaded media is stored and retrievable via URL
Media list view shows uploaded assets with thumbnails
File size and type validation is enforced on upload
Payload Media collection is configured with upload settings
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement image optimization or CDN delivery in this story
Do NOT implement media usage tracking across content

validation.manual_check: Can upload an image and see it in the media list

stories.title: Implement draft and publish workflow
stories.description: Add draft, review, and publish states with role-based transitions.
acceptance_criteria: Content items have a status field with at least draft and published states
Only authorized roles can transition content to published state
Published content is visible through the appropriate surface
Draft content is only visible to editors and admins
Publishing requires reviewer or admin role
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement versioning or revision history
Do NOT implement scheduled publishing — that is a separate story
Do NOT implement approval notifications

validation.manual_check: Can create draft, publish it, verify it appears on the appropriate surface

stories.title: Implement content preview
stories.description: Allow previewing unpublished content in the frontend.
acceptance_criteria: Draft content can be previewed through a unique preview URL or mode
Preview renders content as it would appear when published
Preview is only accessible to authenticated users with appropriate roles
Preview does not leak draft content to public or search engines
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement live editing or inline editing
Do NOT implement preview for multiple content types — start with the primary type

validation.manual_check: Can preview draft content via preview URL while logged in

stories.title: Implement scheduled publishing
stories.description: Create background job to publish content at a scheduled time.
acceptance_criteria: Content items can have a publishAt date/time field
A background job checks for content due for publishing and publishes it
Published content appears on the appropriate surface after the scheduled time
Job runs reliably on a configurable interval
Failed publish attempts are logged and retried
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement a full job queue system — a simple cron-style check is sufficient
Do NOT implement scheduled unpublishing in this story

validation.manual_check: Set publishAt to near future, verify content publishes automatically

stories.title: Build content platform shell
stories.description: Create the content management and delivery shell with editorial navigation.
acceptance_criteria: Admin shell renders with editorial navigation (content, media, settings)
Shell supports both admin and public route groups if public-site capability exists
Content list and detail views have placeholder layouts
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement actual content CRUD — only the navigation shell
Do NOT implement the public site layout in this story if public-site capability exists

validation.manual_check: Admin content shell renders with editorial navigation

stories.title: Setup monitoring and logging
stories.description: Integrate structured logging, health checks, and error tracking across the application.
acceptance_criteria: Structured JSON logging is configured for all backend services
Log levels (debug, info, warn, error) are used consistently
Health check endpoint exists and returns service status
Unhandled errors are caught and logged with stack traces
Database connection health is included in the health check response
Background job execution is logged with start time, duration, and outcome
Request logging includes response time for public-facing routes
scope_boundaries: Do NOT integrate external monitoring services (Datadog, Sentry, etc.) — use structured logs only
Do NOT implement custom dashboards — logs should be queryable via standard tools
Do NOT add performance profiling — focus on operational visibility

validation.manual_check: Health check endpoint returns 200 with service status; logs appear in structured JSON format

stories.title: Implement backups
stories.description: Automate postgres database backups with retention policies and documented restore procedure.
acceptance_criteria: Automated postgres backup script exists and runs successfully
Backup files are stored in a configurable location
Backup retention policy is documented (e.g., keep last 7 daily backups)
Restore procedure is documented and has been tested manually
Backup uses pg_dump with custom format for efficient storage and selective restore
Backup script can run inside or outside the Docker environment
scope_boundaries: Do NOT implement cloud-specific backup solutions (RDS snapshots, etc.) — use portable scripts
Do NOT implement real-time replication — periodic backups are sufficient for MVP
Do NOT implement backup monitoring or alerting — that can be added later

validation.manual_check: Backup file is created; restore script successfully restores a test postgres dump

answers.summary: A public-facing content platform with an integrated CMS supporting content creation, editing, versioning, user management, publishing workflows, multi-language support, and scheduled/background jobs.

assumptions: The public site is being delivered via Next.js frontend as typical for editorial CMS archetype.
CMS administrative capability is paired with full public site delivery for content consumption.
The archetype 'editorial-cms' is well aligned with the project goals of providing a public-facing content platform with CMS.
Multi-language (i18n) and scheduled jobs are required as indicated by user answers.
The frontend stack (Next.js) will handle public site rendering including i18n, previews, and scheduled content publishing.
Backend Payload CMS will manage core CMS capabilities such as content creation, versioning, user roles, and scheduled publishing.
open_questions: Which specific core work features related to the CMS editorial workflow does the user require?
Is multi-language support needed for the site content?
deployment_considerations: Ensure deployment container supports static and dynamic content delivery for both public site and CMS admin interface.
Consider cache strategy for public-facing site performance.
Ensure proper environment variable management for authentication and role features.
Deploying on Docker implies containerization best practices for both backend (Payload CMS) and frontend (Next.js).
Consider internationalization support in frontend and CMS data models.
Ensure scheduled/background jobs infrastructure is configured, potentially with a job queue or cron jobs as part of the deployment.
Verify CDN or caching strategies for public site performance and global multi-language content delivery.

applied_answer_updates.summary: A public-facing content platform with an integrated CMS supporting content creation, editing, versioning, user management, publishing workflows, multi-language support, and scheduled/background jobs.

additional_questions.question: Which core work features are needed? (e.g. content-creation, editing, versioning, user-management, publishing-workflows)
Does the project require multi-language support (internationalization)?
Is the primary audience for this tool internal teams, external clients, or both equally?
Will the application require scheduled or background jobs (e.g. notifications, batch processing)?

core_work_features_list.question: Which core work features are needed? (e.g. content-creation, editing, versioning, user-management, publishing-workflows)

needs_i18n.question: Does the project require multi-language support (internationalization)?

primary_audience.question: Is the primary audience for this tool internal teams, external clients, or both equally?

needs_scheduled_jobs.question: Will the application require scheduled or background jobs (e.g. notifications, batch processing)?

cache-invalidation.option_detail: Trigger Next.js revalidation via webhook after each publish event. Pages update within seconds. Requires a revalidation API route.

preview-vs-production.option_detail: Use Payload's built-in draft mode with Next.js preview cookies. Editors see drafts in-context; public visitors see only published content.

seo-vs-auth-routes.option_detail: Use Next.js route groups: (public) for SEO pages, (app) for admin, (payload) for CMS. Middleware enforces auth only on non-public groups.

content-versioning.option_detail: Skip versioning initially. Editors edit content directly. Simpler but no rollback capability.

i18n-strategy.option_detail: Content, UI labels, and URL structure are all locale-aware. Complete solution but more work upfront.

job-infrastructure.option_detail: Dedicated worker process with its own lifecycle. More reliable. Can scale independently. Needs docker-compose entry.

auth-strategy.option_detail: Classic email/password auth. Use the framework's built-in auth if available (e.g., Payload auth). Simple, no external dependencies.

performance: Public pages should render quickly under normal load.
API responses should target low median latency for public traffic.
Public assets should be cacheable and efficiently delivered.
scalability: System should support horizontal scaling of application servers.
Database must support concurrent authenticated users.
Background job workers must scale independently from interactive application traffic.
security: All authentication flows must enforce secure password hashing.
All external APIs must require authentication or signed requests.
Role and permission boundaries must be enforced consistently.
operational: Localization resources and locale handling must remain consistent across frontend and backend.
Structured logging should be used for all backend services.
Application must run in containerized environments.

philosophy: Use consistent spacing scale and typography hierarchy.
Minimize cognitive load across the application experience.

tokens.tailwind_mapping: Use Tailwind utility classes directly. Only use custom CSS for complex animations or layouts not expressible with utilities.

components.name: ContentCard
components.purpose: Card for displaying content items in lists

components.name: RichTextEditor
components.purpose: Block-based rich text editing for content

components.name: ContentList
components.purpose: Filterable list of content items with status

components.name: ContentStatusBadge
components.purpose: Visual indicator for content workflow state

components.name: PublishControls
components.purpose: Publish, schedule, and unpublish action group

components.name: HeroBlock
components.purpose: Full-width hero section for landing pages

components.name: Footer
components.purpose: Site-wide footer with links and metadata

patterns: Provide clear status indicators for important workflow states.
Use progressive disclosure for advanced options.
Preview mode should match rendered output closely.
Use explicit draft and publish states for editorial workflows.
Design text containers to handle locale expansion gracefully.
Ensure locale switching is easy to find and clearly reflected in the UI.
recommendations: Use Tailwind CSS for styling — map tokens to Tailwind config or utility classes directly.
Maintain a shared component library in src/components/ui/.
Document component usage with prop types and examples.
Follow WAI-ARIA patterns for interactive components (modals, dropdowns, tabs).

observations: Top navigation bar present in all pages with logo left and navigation links center
Buttons on topbar have rounded corners
Three column grid layout in second image content area
Dark mode used in third image with dark background and lighter text
First two images use light background with black text
Search bar visible in second image top right

design_system.reference_style: Modern, clean tech marketing style with a light mode interface featuring black text on white, subtle grey borders, rounded cards, with a contrasting dark mode interface for developer tools showcasing syntax highlights and monospace code.

design_reference.overall_style: Modern, clean tech marketing style with a light mode interface featuring black text on white, subtle grey borders, rounded cards, with a contrasting dark mode interface for developer tools showcasing syntax highlights and monospace code.

observations: Top navigation bar present in all pages with logo left and navigation links center
Buttons on topbar have rounded corners
Three column grid layout in second image content area
Dark mode used in third image with dark background and lighter text
First two images use light background with black text
Search bar visible in second image top right

risks.title: Traffic spikes
risks.impact: The application may become unavailable or slow during peak traffic.
risks.mitigation: Use caching, CDN delivery, and capacity planning for anonymous traffic.

risks.title: Content schema evolution
risks.risk: Content models may evolve after launch.
risks.impact: Schema changes can create migration and compatibility issues.
risks.mitigation: Introduce schema versioning and a migration strategy.

risks.title: Media storage growth
risks.risk: Media assets can grow quickly over time.
risks.impact: Local storage may become insufficient or costly to manage.
risks.mitigation: Use object storage with lifecycle and retention rules.

risks.title: Background job reliability
risks.risk: Automation depends on reliable job execution.
risks.impact: Important workflows may not run at the intended time.
risks.mitigation: Use a durable queue or reliable job persistence strategy.

risks.title: Clock drift
risks.risk: Worker and database clocks may diverge.
risks.impact: Scheduled tasks may run too early or too late.
risks.mitigation: Use UTC timestamps and centralized scheduling logic.

risks.title: Localization consistency
risks.risk: Translations and locale-specific formatting may drift across the application.
risks.impact: Users may experience inconsistent language, formatting, or fallback behavior.
risks.mitigation: Define locale ownership, fallback rules, and validation for translation resources.

directories.purpose: Next.js app router — route groups and pages
Base UI primitives (Button, Input, Modal, etc.)
Shared utilities, helpers, and business logic
Payload Media collection with upload config

entities.name: User
entities.description: System user with authentication credentials and role assignment.

entities.name: Article
entities.description: Editorial content item managed through a draft-to-publish workflow.

entities.name: Category
entities.description: Taxonomy for organizing and filtering content.

entities.name: MediaAsset
entities.description: Uploaded image, document, or file managed in the media library.

business_rules: Only users with reviewer or admin role can approve content for publication.
Published content cannot be edited directly — create a new draft version instead.
Deleting published content requires admin role.
Scheduled publishing requires content to be in approved/in_review state.
The scheduler checks for due content on a configurable interval and publishes automatically.
Media assets must pass file type and size validation before storage.

prd_intelligence.problem_statement: Organizations often need to manage structured editorial content and publish it across digital surfaces without tightly coupling content management to frontend presentation.

personas.name: Content Editor
personas.goal: Create and publish editorial content quickly.

personas.name: Administrator
personas.goal: Manage users, roles, and system configuration.

personas.name: Site Visitor
personas.goal: Consume published content through the public website.

success_metrics: Content publishing time reduced compared to manual workflows.
Editorial users can publish content without developer intervention.
Public pages load under acceptable performance thresholds.

out_of_scope: Complex analytics dashboards not defined in the initial spec.
External integrations not required for core workflow.

assumptions: Both internal teams and external clients will use the system with appropriate access controls.
System will evolve after MVP validation.
Background job infrastructure will be available in the deployment environment.
